36 Mother’s Day Gifts for the Mom Who Has Great Taste

Editor-approved picks for all the amazing moms in your life.

ByJulia Youman

Published OnApril 23, 2026

Photo by Food52

Now that we’re nearing May, we’re popping in with an important reminder: Mother’s Day is coming up fast (May 10th, to be exact).

As someone who works on gift guides year-round, Mother’s Day is always an interesting one for me. Every spring, I start texting moms in my life some version of: “Hey, it’s me again...working on another gift guide.” And honestly? I hear the same thing over and over. They don’t really want more stuff. They want to sleep in. They want their favorite meal. They want someone else to make the plan. They want to spend time with their kids, or have a long lunch with fellow mom friends, or maybe just sit outside in peace.

One of our past contributors, Anne, summed it up perfectly when I asked about her ideal gift: “As far as Mother’s Day goes, I always ask for the same thing: to not lift a finger or do any of the mothering that day.”

Last year I rounded up all of these crowd-sourced answers into one guide, and it ended up being one of our most popular gift stories ever—which is funny, because it was less of a gift guide and more of a Reddit thread proving moms mostly want a day off.

Still, if you’re not showing up empty-handed, or you live far away and want to send something thoughtful, a fun gift absolutely has a place. Moms are amazing. They deserve the brunch and the present.

So below are some things we’re eyeing, genuinely love, or would happily send ourselves. Consider this a guide for moms who love to cook, bake, garden, read cookbooks, and host summer dinner parties. Plus, any of these can be made a little more thoughtful with a handwritten card and fresh flowers.

Our Best Gift Ideas for Moms

Featured Video

See all Food52 videos

1. The Candles Every Stylish Host Owns

Shop Now, $37

While working on our holiday gift guides, I had multiple people known for being incredible hosts and having impeccable taste recommend this exact taper candle brand. Now I can’t stop spotting them in every cool store I walk into. Their classic church candles are timeless, but they also have fun shapes if your mom leans more design-forward.

2. A Serious Farmer’s Market Tote

Shop now, $53

This is the tote we carried for years and the best pick for serious shoppers. Especially if your mom is the type to run in for one thing and somehow come out with ten. I especially love the bottle compartments because I know my own mom never leaves Trader Joe’s empty-handed.

3. Cake Plates for the Art Lover

Shop now, $24+

My mom and favorite section at the Art Institute of Chicago is the Impressionist wing—the Monet water lilies, the Degas ballerinas, all of it. So when I saw this collection inspired by an Impressionist picnic, I had to include it. I love all their tableware, but dessert plates feel like the safest bet.

4. A Pitcher That Doubles as a Vase

Shop now, $64

Everything from East Fork’s etched thistle collection and new spring arrivals is gorgeous, but once again I’m presenting this pitcher I dream of displaying on a shelf. It would be just as pretty filled with tulips as it would on the dinner table.

5. Chocolates as Pretty as Flowers

Shop now, $65

Floral truffles—what more can I say? This box is gorgeous and makes such a lovely gift. I also find a beautiful box of chocolates is always fun to bring to a brunch or dinner so everyone can sample flavors.

6. An Olive Oil Refill

Shop now, $96+

This Brightland x Hill House set is the collaboration we didn’t know we needed. It includes two of Brightland’s signature oils plus gold pour spouts, all wrapped in Hill House's seasonal print. A chic gift from two female-founded brands.

7. A Personalized Kitchen Gift

Shop now, $24+

Last Christmas, I wanted to get my grandma something meaningful and handmade because she always says she has too much stuff. I landed on a jewelry dish engraved with my grandpa’s handwriting, and it was a hit. A custom tea towel has that same thoughtful energy.

8. French Countryside Knives

Shop now, $64

I love Opinel, and we used to sell this exact set a few years back. They’re classic, beautifully made, and the kind of gift someone might not buy for themselves but will use constantly. Also a great gift for the mom who loves all things French.

9. A Table Runner for Spring Hosting

Shop now, $80

A table runner is one of those little luxuries that instantly makes dinner feel more special. This embroidered version is especially lovely for spring and summer hosting.

10. The Cookbook We’d Buy Ourselves

Shop now, $30

We absolutely adore Lucie from her beautiful bakery, From Lucie, where she makes the most gorgeous cakes. We were lucky enough to have her in the kitchen recently while she was on tour for her new book, where she shares recipes and decorating secrets. If you want to preview it, she shared a chamomile cake with strawberry sumac jam that we still think about.

11. A Scenic Dutch Oven

Shop now, $135

We’re obsessed with all of Goldilocks’ limited-edition Dutch ovens and have been eyeing this new one hard. It’s a collaboration with New York–based illustrator Joana Avillez, inspired by Portuguese tiles and storybook houses. The kind of pot you’ll want to leave out on the stove.

12. Serveware for the Vegetable Lover

Shop now, $105

We love anything vegetable-inspired, especially serveware. So much so that we practically instituted a temporary ban on writing about pretty plates around here, but I still had to include this cabbage platter from Bordallo Pinheiro.

13. A Fancy Pantry Upgrade

Shop now, $35

We recently tried this at the office and it’s some of the smoothest jam I’ve had in a long time. Plus, we’re obsessed with rhubarb and finally got it back in New York, so the timing feels right.

14. Flowers That Never Wilt

Shop now, $25

An editor from another publication recently told me one of their best-performing gift guide items was Lego flower sets. At first I was surprised, then it made total sense. It’s kind of the perfect gift—slightly crafty, decorative, and lasts forever. I also happen to love these from the MoMA Design Store because you can pick from an array of famous art styles (Van Gogh, Klimt, etc).

15. An Edible Gift Set

Shop now, $28

Bonne Maman makes some of the cutest gift sets that actually come in around $30, which makes this a great budget-friendly pick. This one is especially fun because it includes limited-edition flavors like Strawberry Guava and Pineapple Passion Fruit you won’t usually find on grocery shelves.

16. The Bouquet She’ll Actually Use

shop now, $70

I always love a little wordplay and thinking beyond traditional flowers. Nice pastry flour also qualifies, in my opinion. But another fun option is a hand cream bouquet like this one from L’Occitane, which is mom-tested and approved many times over. My mom asks for it every year, and when I brought some into the office recently, multiple moms were delighted.

17. For the Mom Who Throws a Proper Picnic

Shop now, $109

Growing up, there are a lot of things my mom did that stuck with me, but her picnic setups are high on the list. In the summer, we’d go to outdoor concerts with charcuterie, fruit, and snacks packed beautifully. This brings that same spirit, but upgraded.

18. A Charming Upgrade for Breakfast

Shop now, $70

Egg cups have been on my personal wish list for the past year, so I’m always noticing especially charming ones. These hand-painted ceramic versions would make breakfast feel instantly more fun.

19. A Salt-and-Honey Combo She’ll Love

Shop now, $33+

A dream gift for the person who loves pantry luxuries. Pick a few salts, a honey, maybe add olive oil, and suddenly you’ve built a very thoughtful little bundle.

20. A Cheese Tasting

Shop now, $52

I went to Napa in the fall and did a Cabot cheddar tasting that was so fun. We built little boards with fresh fruit, honey, and pairings, then tasted each cheese and compared notes. It reminded me how great an interactive gift can be, and this would be such a fun one to plan with your mom.

21. A Chocolate Tasting

Shop now, $40

How beautiful is Dandelion Chocolate? When a coworker sent it to me, I spent a full half hour scrolling the site and reading about how they make everything. I love this tasting set because it comes with notes and a guide, which could be fun to do with your mom.

22. For the Sentimental Gardener

Shop now, $65

Gardenheir has so many good gift ideas, including this flower press set. Great for the mom who loves to garden or arrange a stellar bouquet. Bonus points if you gift it with flowers.

23. A Chic Shear Replacement

Shop now, $19

Highly recommended by someone involved in a garden club year-round, which I consider a source I trust deeply. Plus, many moms I’ve spoken to say their Mother’s Day dream is simply to spend the day in the garden, so if you can’t be there in person, send some beautiful tools.

24. A Spice Upgrade

Shop now, $55

If your mom is really into cooking, Burlap & Barrel’s chef spice kit is such a fun gift. It includes some great wildcards and harder-to-find spices that can instantly shake up her usual rotation. It’s also a brand many restaurants and chefs use for a reason, so it feels a little like bringing the pro pantry home.

25. French Cheese Knives

Shop now, $13+

Erin French from The Lost Kitchen curates the best shop, and if your mom loves her show or restaurant, now you can bring a little piece of it home. I love these colorful French cheese knives, but there’s also beautiful cookware and linen tea towels worth browsing.

26. The Splurge for Moms Who Bake

Shop now, $300

If you really want to splurge and your mom loves to bake, may I present Le Creuset’s latest release: a new shape of their signature bread oven. We were immediately interested after seeing favorite creators bake with it, and the new Riviera summer colorway is especially good.

Shop now, $22

If you’d rather go smaller, I’m a huge fan of their mini cocottes. They’re perfect for garlic confit, lava cakes, baked dips, or really anything small that needs to go in the oven. I also think they make some of the best gifts, especially in the same summery colorway.

27. Vintage-Inspired Serveware

Shop now, $38

Something I’ve been loving to gift lately is vintage or vintage-inspired serveware. Last Christmas I got my sister, who loves to bake, a vintage cake server from England off Etsy, and it’s already been heavily used. I love these salad servers from Magnolia because they feel classic but a little different.

Shop now, $30

28. Linen Napkins for Spring Tables

Shop now, $96

Madre Linen’s Primavera collection is a shoe-in gift for us. We also love their ribbons and table linens if you want to build out a full hosting set.

Shop now, $28+

29. A Colorful Moka Pot

Shop now, $70

When I was in Italy last fall, I stayed in Parma for a week and every morning I’d walk past the Bialetti store and stare in the window. I wanted to bring one home so badly, but had no room in my suitcase. Ever since, I’ve thought what a good gift they make. I dream of the Dolce & Gabbana version, but lately this blue one has caught my eye.

30. A New Way to Brew Tea

Shop now, $35

If you follow my other guides, you know I love Tea Forte as a gift because I’ve had real-life parents test it out and the feedback is always strong. Their new collection with Draper James feels especially fitting for Mother’s Day. I’d also recommend pairing with a matching tea sampler for a fun set.

31. A Cheerful Upgrade for Travel

Shop now, $44

With vegetable and fruit prints, I love these Baggu bags. Baggu feels like one of those universally mom-approved brands for a reason; they’re lightweight, durable, easy to clean, and somehow always cute. Refresh her travel or errand setup with something cheerful and genuinely useful.

32. A Pretty Wine Glass

Shop now, $88

For as long as I can remember, I’ve dreamed of Juliska products. Maybe because the cool French store in my hometown carried them, or maybe because my mom always loved them. Either way, these glasses feel timeless. You don’t need to commit to a full set either. A single beautiful glass for her favorite wine, spritz, or evening drink can feel just as special.

33. An Herbal Tea Sampler

Shop now, $42

One of the many Brooklyn-based brands we love is Raazi Tea. You can now find their jasmine tea at Anthropologie, but I’m especially into the herbal sampler. The packaging alone makes it Mother’s Day-worthy.

34. A Candle Holder Refresh

Shop now, $14+

Hand-poured from a woman-owned brand with ethically sourced materials. Also the best kind of votive holder—the one that throws beautiful light around the room.

35. Recipes to Cook Through Together

Shop now, $21

We had Sarah Ahn stop by last year with her mom to make their signature kimchi grilled cheese from Umma. It’s the kind of cookbook filled with recipes, stories, and dishes you’ll actually want to cook together.

Shop now, $27

Another lovely option comes from the mother-daughter duo behind a cooking store and school in France: French at Heart. Filled with approachable French recipes (even for anyone typically intimidated by the category) it’s the kind of cookbook you’ll actually cook from. Plus, the photography is beautiful enough to flip through on its own.

36. Bud Vases for Every Corner of the House

Shop now, $44

When I went to Venice a few years ago, I did a day trip to Murano, basically the land of blown glass. There’s a museum full of incredible chandeliers, and every shop sells beautiful glass pieces. I always regretted not bringing home more. Then while scrolling Maison Flaneur, I found these bud vases and was sold. I love bud vases as gifts because they’re pretty, easy to store, and perfect for flowers in every corner of the house.



Our best tips for eating thoughtfully and living joyfully, right in your inbox.
